title: SEC2: canonicalize_display_name — no genuine efficiency issues
description:
Efficiency review of canonicalize_display_name in relay/src/ws.rs (lines 53-67):

**Analysis:**

1. **NFC normalization (line 54)**: s.nfc().collect() is acceptable — one allocation for the iterator result.

2. **BIDI_CONTROLS linear scan (line 55)**:
   - 11 elements, checked per character via .contains(&c)
   - Worst-case: 32 chars × 11 comparisons = 352 comparisons
   - No concern for ≤32 char input size

3. **ZWS_CHARS linear scan (line 58)**:
   - 3 elements, checked per character
   - Worst-case: 32 chars × 3 = 96 comparisons
   - Negligible cost

4. **Multiple passes (4 total)**:
   - NFC iterate+collect (line 54)
   - .chars().any() for BIDI check (line 55)
   - .chars().filter() for ZWS strip (line 58)
   - .chars().count() for length check (line 63)
   - Combining BIDI and ZWS passes would save one iteration, but readability benefit outweighs negligible perf gain

**Conclusion**: All O(n) passes over ≤32 char strings are acceptable. Array linear scans on 3–11 elements are fine. No optimization needed.
close_reason: Efficiency review complete: all operations are O(n) over ≤32 char strings, no optimization needed
